preview.tournamentOverview: TPC Twin Cities is a parkland layout that rewards ball-striking and short-game control over raw distance. Approach play (25.8%) and total strokes gained (22.8%) carry the heaviest course weights, while putting (16.8%) and around-the-green (13.3%) remain critical for scrambling on firm, undulating greens. The 3M Open offers a strong field and solid FedExCup points, making it a key stop for players looking to climb the standings before the playoffs.
preview.modelExplainer: Our model weights approach play most heavily at 25.8%, followed by total SG at 22.8% and putting at 16.8%, reflecting the course's demand for precision iron play and reliable scoring. Off-the-tee (11.4%) and around-the-green (13.3%) receive moderate emphasis, while driving accuracy (3.9%) and bogey avoidance (3.0%) are deprioritized. This weighting structure favors players who consistently hit greens and convert putts rather than pure bombers.
preview.pickApproach: We tier selections by blending tournament-specific SG ranks with baseline power rankings to identify value. Top-tier bets target players with elite approach and total SG marks who also rank inside the top 30 in power. Mid-tier and long-shot wagers focus on golfers whose course-fit SG spikes outweigh weaker overall rankings, seeking mispriced upside in outrights and top-20 markets.

bullets: Model ranks Scheffler #2 with sgTotal=1.868, second only to Knapp.
Strong SG OTT=0.623 and SG ATG=0.618 align with course weights of SG OTT 11.4% and SG ATG 13.3%.
Recent form shows Top 5 at The Open and 6 rounds in two weeks, yet model still places him #2 overall.
Price looks like value given powerRank=2 and sgTotal=1.868 versus market pricing.
outrights.risk: Heavy recent workload of 6 rounds in two weeks could impact stamina.
angles: FedExCup rank #1 provides motivation despite Top 5 Open finish.

article.title: 3M Open Model Targets at TPC Twin Cities
article.dek: Model ranks Scheffler, Matsuyama, and Højgaard as primary outright options while Knapp leads the field in sgTotal at 2.002.
article.introduction: The tournament model places Jake Knapp at rank 1 with sgTotal 2.002 and Scottie Scheffler at rank 2 with sgTotal 1.868. Course weights emphasize SG APP at 25.8% and SG Total at 22.8%. Post-Open workload and FedExCup context are factored for the 144-player field starting 2026-07-23.

sections.heading: Tournament Overview
sections.body: The model ranks the top 20 players by combined power and tournament rank, with Jake Knapp at tournamentRank 1 and powerRank 1, followed by Scottie Scheffler at tournamentRank 2 and powerRank 2.

sections.heading: Course Factors
sections.body: TPC Twin Cities weights SG APP at 25.8%, SG Total at 22.8%, SG PUT at 16.8%, and SG ATG at 13.3%, with SG OTT at 11.4% and Driving Accuracy at 3.9%.

sections.heading: Post-Open Workload and Motivation
sections.body: Scottie Scheffler logged six rounds in two weeks after a top-5 Open finish and holds FedExCup rank 1. Kurt Kitayama played eight rounds and sits at FedExCup rank 32. Jordan Smith and Corey Conners each logged eight rounds with FedExCup ranks 66 and 58.

sections.heading: Outright Targets
sections.body: Scottie Scheffler (rank 2) carries heavy recent workload of six rounds in two weeks. Hideki Matsuyama (rank 4) shows SG OTT -0.099. Rasmus Højgaard (rank 10) posts SG ATG -0.273. Jason Day (rank 11) records SG APP -0.352.

sections.heading: Top-10 Targets
sections.body: Jake Knapp (rank 1) leads with sgTotal 2.002 but faces putting regression risk from 1.285. Scottie Scheffler (rank 2) shows sgApp 0.08. Sudarshan Yellamaraju (rank 3) posts sgAtG -0.188. Hideki Matsuyama (rank 4) has sgOTT -0.099. David Lipsky (rank 5) records sgOTT -0.31.

sections.heading: Top-20 Targets
sections.body: Maverick McNealy (rank 6) carries sgPutt 0.286. Austin Smotherman (rank 7) shows sgPutt -0.494. Andrew Putnam (rank 8) posts sgOTT -0.341. Sam Stevens (rank 9) records sgPutt -0.218. Rasmus Højgaard (rank 10) has sgAtG -0.273. Jason Day (rank 11) shows sgApp -0.352. Gary Woodland (rank 15) posts sgAtG -0.397.

sections.heading: Players to Approach Cautiously
sections.body: Austin Smotherman (rank 7) enters with sgPutt -0.494. Gary Woodland (rank 15) shows sgAtG -0.397. These negative strokes-gained figures sit among the lowest in the top-20 model rows.

article.conclusion: Primary outright tier: Scottie Scheffler, Hideki Matsuyama. Primary top-10 tier: Jake Knapp, Scottie Scheffler. Primary top-20 tier: Maverick McNealy, Andrew Putnam.

methodologyNotes: Picks are generated from a course-weighted strokes-gained model, cross-checked against the official PGA TOUR field.
Post-Open Championship, Scottish Open, and FedExCup context is included because this is the tournament immediately following The Open Championship.
dataLimitations: Weather and tee-time data are not available in this pipeline; the article does not make weather- or tee-time-specific claims.
